perturbation.original_text: 1.02. AIRSOPURE hereby grants to You the exclusive right to solicit customers for the Products by direct mail advertising, or other approved means, but not including the World Wide Web (Internet) nor by printed catalogues, in 1 the Exclusive Territory described above and in Exhibit A. Other AIRSOPURE franchisees will not be permitted to solicit customers for Products by advertising in Your Exclusive Territory. Likewise, You may not target or solicit customers for Products by advertising in other Franchisees respective Exclusive Territories. Exclusive Territories will not overlap into other Exclusive Territories. You shall not purposely solicit sales and service to customers located outside Your Exclusive Territory, provided such activity in not within an assigned Exclusive Territory. You have been granted an exclusive trade area by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.02. AIRSOPURE hereby grants to You the exclusive right to solicit customers for the Products by direct mail advertising, or other approved means, including the World Wide Web (Internet) and by printed catalogues, in the Exclusive Territory described above and in Exhibit A. Other AIRSOPURE franchisees will be permitted to solicit customers for Products by advertising in Your Exclusive Territory. Likewise, You may target or solicit customers for Products by advertising in other Franchisees respective Exclusive Territories. Exclusive Territories will overlap into other Exclusive Territories. You shall purposely solicit sales and service to customers located outside Your Exclusive Territory, provided such activity is within an assigned Exclusive Territory. You have been granted a non-exclusive trade area by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces contradictions to several aspects of the agreement regarding the Exclusive Territory. The original text states that the franchisee has an exclusive right to solicit customers, excluding internet and catalogs, and prohibits other franchisees from advertising in their territory, granting an 'exclusive trade area'. The modification allows solicitation through all means, permits other franchisees to advertise in the territory, allows territory overlap, and changes the trade area to 'non-exclusive'. This directly contradicts the original intent of granting an exclusive territory, creating uncertainty regarding the franchisee's rights and potentially leading to disputes over market share and advertising permissions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: AIRSOPURE grants to You, and You accept from AIRSOPURE, the right and license to operate an AIRSOPURE Center (or the "Franchise") for the sale or lease of AIRSOPURE's exclusive line of Products or at a location in the Exclusive Territory to be approved in writing by AIRSOPURE and listed in attached Exhibit A (the "Exclusive Territory and Center Location"), to purchase Products from AIRSOPURE or its affiliates for resale at the Center to customers in the Exclusive Territory, and to use the Licensed Marks only in connection with the operation of the Franchise in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. AIRSOPURE grants the Franchise to You hereunder in reliance upon Your agreement to at all times operate and manage the Franchise faithfully, honestly and diligently in strict conformance with AIRSOPURE's operating procedures and specifications, as set forth herein and as otherwise from time to time communicated to You, using Your best efforts to promote and enhance the performance and operation of the Franchise.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the concept of an 'Exclusive Territory' as it permits other franchisees to solicit customers within Your territory, allows territory overlap, allows soliciting outside the territory, and makes the trade area 'non-exclusive'. This conflicts with the contradicted text, which grants the right to operate in an 'Exclusive Territory' and purchase products for resale to customers 'in the Exclusive Territory'. The original text established a specific, protected area, while the modified text eliminates this exclusivity, creating a clear inconsistency in the rights and obligations of the franchisee.

perturbation.original_text: B. You shall pay to AIRSOPURE a continuing non-refundable royalty fee on a monthly basis of 2 5% of Your total monthly gross sales, as defined below. This fee is due by the 7th of the month for the preceding month.
perturbation.changed_text: B. You shall pay to AIRSOPURE a royalty fee on a monthly basis of 5% of Your total monthly gross sales, as defined below. This fee is due by the 7th of the month for the preceding month. This royalty fee is potentially refundable, at the sole discretion of Airsopure, based on franchisee performance and adherence to company standards.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states the royalty fee is 'non-refundable,' establishing a definite financial obligation for the franchisee. The modified version introduces ambiguity by stating the fee is 'potentially refundable, at the sole discretion of Airsopure'. This directly contradicts the original statement, creating uncertainty about the franchisee's actual financial obligations. The conditionality tied to 'franchisee performance' is vague and subjective, making enforcement unpredictable and prone to disputes. It contradicts the original term that was clearly non-refundable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You shall pay to AIRSOPURE an initial franchise fee of $25,000.00 upon execution of this Agreement. You agree that the initial franchise fee represents payment for the initial grant of the Franchise rights and license granted herein, shall be fully earned upon execution of this Agreement, and the said fee will not be refunded under any circumstances unless otherwise specifically set forth herein. You must find a suitable Center site within 90 days of signing the Franchise Agreement, unless we mutually agree otherwise. If we cannot agree on a Center location, within 4 months, we may a) extend your search time, b) exchange your territory, or c) terminate your Franchise and refund up to 70% of your Franchise Fee, at our sole discretion. There are no refunds under any other circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the royalty fee is 'non-refundable,' creating a clear and definite obligation. The changed text makes the royalty fee 'potentially refundable, at the sole discretion of Airsopure'. This contradicts the initial condition of non-refundability. While the contradicted text pertains to a franchise fee and outlines specific refund conditions, the modification introduces a condition of potential refundability which then is incongruent with the first stated condition in the original text.

perturbation.original_text: 14.02. Except for violations of this Agreement listed in Section 13.01 above, or violations specifically provided for elsewhere in this Agreement, You shall have 30 days from receipt from AIRSOPURE of a written Notice of Termination (citing the reason(s) therefor) within which to remedy any default listed in this Section 13.02, or any other violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 14.02. Even for violations of this Agreement listed in Section 13.01 above, You shall have 30 days from receipt from AIRSOPURE of a written Notice of Termination (citing the reason(s) therefor) within which to remedy any default listed in this Section 13.02, or any other violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text of Section 14.02 explicitly states an exception: violations listed in Section 13.01, or specifically provided for elsewhere, do not grant the franchisee a 30-day cure period before termination. By removing the "Except for" clause, the modified text now implies that all violations, including those in Section 13.01, are subject to the 30-day cure period. This directly contradicts the original intent, creating uncertainty and potential disputes as to whether the franchisee has a right to cure certain defaults before termination. This in-text change directly contradicts the original exceptions that there are to Section 13.01 and now introduces a contradiction where one did not exist.
perturbation.contradicted_text: AIRSOPURE may, at its option, terminate this Agreement and all rights granted hereunder, without affording You any opportunity to cure the defaults, effective immediately upon receipt of notice by You, upon the occurrence of any of the following: A. You become insolvent or makes a general assignment for the benefit of creditors; or if a 12 petition in bankruptcy is filed by You or such a petition is filed against and consented to by You; or if You are adjudicated a bankrupt; or if You are unable to pay commercial debts as they become due. B. You (or a principal shareholder if the Franchisee is a corporation) is convicted of a felony or any other crime or offense that is reasonably likely, in the sole opinion of AIRSOPURE, to adversely affect the goodwill or reputation of AIRSOPURE or the Licensed Marks. C. A judgment or consent decree is entered against You (or a principal shareholder if the Franchisee is a corporation) in a case involving allegations of fraud, racketeering, unfair or deceptive trade practices or similar allegations which, in AIRSOPURE's judgment, are likely to adversely affect AIRSOPURE, its Products, the Licensed Marks or the goodwill associated therewith. D. You or any partner or shareholder in You transfers any rights or obligations under this Agreement or any interest in You or in the Franchise to any third Party without AIRSOPURE's prior written consent. E. You intentionally disclose the contents of the Operations Manual or&bbsp;other trade secrets or confidential information provided to You by AIRSOPURE to any unauthorized person or fails to exercise reasonable care to prevent such disclosure. F. You maintain false books or records of the Franchise or knowingly make any material false statements or omission to AIRSOPURE in connection with Your application for the franchise granted herein or in connection with any reports submitted to AIRSOPURE, including without limitation the understatement of gross sales by more than 2%. G. You fail to commence business within 4 months following the execution of this Agreement. H. You (and Your manager) fail to attend any scheduled training program which AIRSOPURE has indicated is mandatory. I. You operate the Franchise in such a manner which causes a threat or danger to public health or safety. J. You receive 3 or more notices of default of this Agreement from AIRSOPURE for violations under Section 14.02 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excludes violations in Section 13.01 from the 30-day cure period. The changed text removes this exclusion, implying that all violations, including those in Section 13.01, are subject to the cure period. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which allows AIRSOPURE to terminate the agreement immediately for specific violations (listed A-J) without any opportunity for the franchisee to cure the defaults.
